chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْإِشَارَةِ وَ النَّصِّ عَلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 72 – The Indication and the wordings upon Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/344)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/344)

parts.text: كَانَتْ أُمُّهُ مِنْ وُلْدِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام )

text: بَعَثَ إِلَيْنَا أَبُو الْحَسَنِ مُوسَى ( عليه السلام ) فَجَمَعَنَا ثُمَّ قَالَ لَنَا أَ تَدْرُونَ لِمَ دَعَوْتُكُمْ فَقُلْنَا لَا فَقَالَ اشْهَدُوا أَنَّ ابْنِي هَذَا وَصِيِّي وَ الْقَيِّمُ بِأَمْرِي وَ خَلِيفَتِي مِنْ بَعْدِي مَنْ كَانَ لَهُ عِنْدِي دَيْنٌ فَلْيَأْخُذْهُ مِنِ ابْنِي هَذَا وَ مَنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ عِنْدِي عِدَةٌ فَلْيُنْجِزْهَا مِنْهُ وَ مَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ بُدٌّ مِنْ لِقَائِي فَلَا يَلْقَنِي إِلَّا بِكِتَابِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Mihran, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Muhammad Bin Al Fuzayl who said,

‘Al-Makhzoumy narrated to me, and it was so that his paternal uncle was from the children of Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup>. He said, ‘Abu Al-Hassan Musa<sup>asws</sup> sent for me. So we gathered ourselves, then said to us: ‘Do you know why I<sup>asws</sup> invited you all?’ So we said, ‘No’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Bear witness that this son<sup>asws</sup> of mine<sup>asws</sup> is my<sup>asws</sup> successor<sup>asws</sup>, and the establisher of my<sup>asws</sup> commands, and my<sup>asws</sup> Caliph after me<sup>asws</sup>. The one who has a debt for him with me<sup>asws</sup>, so let him take it from this son<sup>asws</sup> of mine<sup>asws</sup>, and the one who had a promise for him with me<sup>asws</sup>, so let him have it fulfilled from him<sup>asws</sup>, and the one for whom it is inevitable that he must meet me<sup>asws</sup>, so he should not come to meet me<sup>asws</sup> except by his<sup>asws</sup> letter (Written authorisation)’.
en.sarwar: 7. Ahmad ibn Mihran has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali from Muhammad ibn Fudayl who has said that al-Makhdhumi whose mother was one of the children of Ja‘far ibn abu Talib has narrated the following. “Abu al-Hassan Musa (a.s.) once called all of us to see him. We all gathered and then he said to us, “Do you know why have I called you?” We said, “We do not know.” He then said, “Bear testimony that this my son is the executor of my will, the director of my affairs and the succeeding Imam (a.s.). Whoever has a loan due on me should demand from my son, this one. To whoever I may have promised anything should also acquire from him. Whoever must see me must not come to see me with writing from him.”
